prs.title: Add Real-time Satellite Pass Notifications
prs.body:
# Summary
Implemented a sleek, non-intrusive notification system that alerts users when satellites pass overhead. The component runs silently in the background and displays elegant slide-in notifications with satellite pass details.
Features

Background Monitoring: Continuously tracks satellite positions using existing TLE data and worker logic
Real-time Notifications: Displays notifications when satellites pass within detection radius (1000km)
Cross-page Functionality: Fixed positioning ensures notifications appear regardless of current page
Auto-dismiss: Notifications automatically hide after 5 seconds with manual close option
Browser Integration: Sends both in-app and native browser notifications when permissions are granted

## Technical Details

- Uses existing satellite prediction worker and TLE data processing
- Maintains user location tracking and satellite pass calculations
- Displays elevation, distance, and timestamp for each satellite pass

## User Experience

- Provides key satellite information (name, elevation, distance, time)
- Non-blocking design that doesn't interfere with main application usage
- Responsive design works across different screen sizes

### Preview

![preview](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c0bcf21e-d3e5-42b5-81f8-368303d9d387)

prs.title: Make a satellite Tracking Feature
prs.body:
## Make a Satellite Tracking Feature (#55)

### Features Added:
- Integrated a 3D Earth model using Cesium
- Implemented live tracking of satellite positions using TLE data
- Added ability to select and follow individual satellites in real time
- Enabled rendering and display of satellite entities in orbit

### Requirements:
- Set `VITE_CESIUM_TOKEN` in your `.env` file to enable Cesium rendering

### Known Issues (WIP):
- Orbital trails are not forming fully closed loops yet
- Some TLE data may be outdated, causing slight mismatches between predicted and actual satellite positions

### Preview:
[preview](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2f36d04f-9ae9-4f47-89bc-852cf8e76491)

prs.title: Add Firebase authentication with email/password
prs.body:
## Pull Request: Auth

### Summary of Changes

- **Authentication**
  - Implemented robust login and signup with Firebase Auth.
  - Added client-side validation for email and password strength.
  - Improved error handling: mapped Firebase error codes to user-friendly messages, including new codes like `auth/invalid-credential`.
  - Added loading state and spinner during authentication actions.

issues.title: Improve Visual Design of the Sign-Up and Log-In Pages

labels.name: Theme: Design
labels.description: Issues related to UI design, branding, etc. [managed]

labels.name: Needs: Triage
labels.description: This issue needs triage. The team needs to decide who should own it, what to do, by when. [managed]

labels.name: Priority: 4
labels.description: An issue, but should be worked on when no other pressing work can be done. [managed]

labels.name: Lead: @mekarpeles
labels.description: Issues overseen by Mek (Staff: Program Lead) [managed]

labels.name: Needs: Response
labels.description: Issues which require feedback from lead

labels.name: Type: Design Proposal
labels.description: Proposing a design and soliciting feedback + approval

issues.body: ## Description, Patron Story The current Open Library sign-up and login pages have very minimal visual design, which may feel plain or uninviting for new users who are creating an account for the first time. The goal of this design update is to improve the visual hierarchy, clarity, and overall friendliness of the page while staying consistent with Open Library’s existing design language. This change introduces: - A clearer “Create an account” introduction section - A short list of key benefits (borrow books, track reading, etc.) - Improved spacing and typography for readability - Placement of the Open Library logo to strengthen identity - A small footer note clarifying the page’s connection to the Internet Archive No functional changes are introduced — only visual improvements. ## Relevant Designs ## Feedback Questions I would love feedback from the community and design stakeholders on the following: 1) Do you like the overall look and feel? 2) Does the page feel more welcoming? 3) Are the logo size and placement okay? 4) Any changes you’d make to the text or bullets? 5) Does the spacing feel right or too empty anywhere? 6) Anything that feels off or could be improved? All feedback — small or big — is super helpful! ## Decisions ### Stakeholders

issues.title: Create standardized GitHub issue templates for consistent reporting and tracking
issues.body:
### Description:
Currently, issues are being created in varying formats, which makes it harder to maintain consistency and quickly understand their purpose or required actions.Introducing structured issue templates will streamline communication, reduce ambiguity, and improve the overall developer workflow.

## Expected Behaviour:

- Contributors should see predefined templates when creating new issues.

- Each template should prompt for key details such as current behaviour, expected behaviour, steps to reproduce, proposed solution, and   screenshots (if relevant).

- Different templates should exist for:

     1) Feature Requests

     2) Bug Reports

     3) Enhancements / Refactors

     4) Documentation Updates

## Course of Action:

- Add an .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/ directory in the repository.

- Create Markdown-based templates for each category with relevant sections, e.g.:

      1) feature_request.md
          
      2) bug_report.md
          
      3) enhancement.md
          
      4) docs_update.md


- Ensure the templates use clear section headings and short guidance text for contributors.

issues.title: Implement TanStack Query for Astronomy News Fetching
issues.body:
### Description:
Currently, the astronomy news fetching logic is handled manually, which results in repetitive data fetching, no caching, and limited refetch control. Integrating TanStack Query will simplify the logic, standardize the data flow, and introduce built-in caching and background refetching mechanisms.

## Expected Behaviour:

- Astronomy news data should be fetched using TanStack Query hooks instead of manual useEffect + fetch logic.

- Data should be cached efficiently and automatically refetched at defined intervals or on focus.

- Loading and error states should be handled gracefully with built-in query states (isLoading, isError, etc.).

## Course of Action:

- Install and configure TanStack Query (if not already part of the project).

- Replace manual fetch logic with useQuery hooks for astronomy news.

issues.title: Start periodic fetching of satellite data from API to maintain up-to-date orbits
issues.body:
### Description:
Currently, most satellite data being displayed is outdated, resulting in inaccurate orbit information.
To resolve this, implement periodic fetching of fresh data from the external API at regular intervals (e.g., every few minutes).

## Expected Behaviour:

The system should automatically retrieve and update satellite data from the API on a defined schedule. Displayed orbital parameters and visualisations should always reflect the latest available information.

## Course of Action:

- Implement an interval-based fetch mechanism to periodically call the API. Update the local data with the new data on each successful fetch. Ensure smooth transitions so users always see the most recent orbits without noticeable lag or flicker. Add error handling and retry logic for network/API failures.

- Creating said API as excessive refetching can cause the website to get banned and no unified way to track a last fetched timestamp across users calls for a lightweight self developed API.

PS: I would like to work on this :)

issues.title: Add a user preferences/settings menu to customize satellite notification and tracking behaviour
issues.body:
### Description:
Currently, users receive satellite overpass notifications by default, with no way to manage or disable them. This limits flexibility for users who may want to control the type and frequency of alerts or adjust visual and tracking preferences.

## Expected Behaviour:

- Users should be able to open a Preferences or Settings window from the main interface.

- They can toggle satellite overpass notifications on/off.

- Additional optional controls could include:

     - Setting a notification radius (e.g., only notify for satellites within X km).

     - Selecting which satellites to track or ignore.

     - Adjusting visualization preferences (e.g., reduced animations, simplified UI mode).

     - All preferences should persist across sessions (stored locally or via user profiles).

## Course of Action:

- Design a minimal Preferences window or settings drawer accessible via the main menu or profile section.

- Implement persistent storage for settings (e.g., localStorage, IndexedDB).

Dark theme missing on non editor pages

issues.title: [FEATURE] Open Recent sub menu under File menu
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
It’s sometimes hard to quickly reopen diagrams I was recently working on, since I need to manually browse for them each time.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Add an “Open Recent” dropdown under the File menu (like VS Code) that lists recently opened diagrams for faster access.

**Additional context**
This would improve workflow efficiency, especially when switching between multiple diagrams.

ps: I would like to help build this if possible.
